spk_a.transcript: 你是英雄是好汉就敢做敢认。
主子，海瑞就是这个赵贞吉指使的。

spk_b.transcript: 海瑞是臣主管的户部主事。

qa_result.question: What is the overall stance of the speaker who said "你是英雄是好汉就敢做敢认。" in the whole conversation?
options: A. Rebukes Zhao Zhenji for refusing to take responsibility when encountering problems, and advocates a thorough investigation of all relevant personnel behind the Hai Rui incident
B. Always defends Zhao Zhenji, and believes that he has no crime of deceiving the emperor
C. Considers the remarks calling him a hero and a true man to be treasonous, and pleads with the emperor to withdraw such improper remarks
D. Cannot be determined
qa_result.answer_text: Rebukes Zhao Zhenji for refusing to take responsibility when encountering problems, and advocates a thorough investigation of all relevant personnel behind the Hai Rui incident
rationale: A. ground truth
B. hallucination
C. wrong speaker
D. unknown

qa_result.question: What changes does the stance of the speaker who once said "你是英雄是好汉就敢做敢认。" undergo throughout the whole conversation?
options: A. From angrily rebuking Zhao Zhenji for failing to take responsibility → voluntarily requests the Emperor to order a thorough investigation into relevant personnel → respectfully complies with the Emperor's instructions afterwards
B. From refusing to plead guilty from the start → voluntarily takes full responsibility for all charges → finally states that he only has the Emperor as his backer
C. From being amiable to all people at the beginning → consistently opposes the thorough investigation of personnel related to Hai Rui → firmly stands on Zhao Zhenji's side in the end
D. It cannot be determined
qa_result.answer_text: From angrily rebuking Zhao Zhenji for failing to take responsibility, to voluntarily requesting the Emperor to order a thorough investigation into relevant personnel, and then to respectfully complying with the Emperor's instructions afterwards
rationale: A. ground truth
B. wrong speaker
C. hallucination
D. unknown

qa_result.question: What is the overall stance of the speaker in the following single-speaker audio clip throughout the whole conversation?
options: A. Voluntarily take responsibility for the charges related to the Hai Rui incident, do not implicate other court officials, and clearly state that he takes the emperor as the only mentor and patron, and never forms any private faction
B. Claim that he is the mastermind behind Hai Rui's serious crime of defying the sovereign, and demand the emperor to execute him immediately
C. Intend to confess all his associated allies in the imperial court related to the Hai Rui incident, and cooperate with the emperor to thoroughly investigate all relevant personnel
D. Unable to judge
qa_result.answer_text: Voluntarily take responsibility for the charges related to the Hai Rui incident, do not implicate other court officials, and explicitly state that he regards only the emperor as his mentor and backer, and has no private faction at all
rationale: A. ground truth, the speaker explicitly admitted that he bears two counts of responsibility for the Hai Rui incident, and later publicly stated that all his official promotions came from the emperor's promotion, he has no private faction and will not implicate other court officials, which is his core stance throughout the whole process

B. wrong speaker, this statement is the demand of another speaker in the conversation who tries to coerce the target speaker to plead guilty, it is not the target speaker's own stance, which mismatches the view of other speakers
C. hallucination, the conversation has no content mentioning that the speaker voluntarily confesses his accomplices at all, which is fabricated false information
D. unknown, there is clear evidence from multiple segments of the conversation to support the speaker's core stance, so it does not belong to the situation that cannot be judged

qa_result.question: What is the change of stance of the speaker talking from 17.3s to 19.7s in the audio throughout the whole conversation?
options: A. From being frightened and flustered when facing the emperor's inquiry at the beginning, passively responding and shirking responsibility, to gradually becoming resolute and tough afterwards, voluntarily taking responsibility to clear his connection with other court officials, and fully demonstrating his loyalty by standing on the side of imperial power
B. He tried to shift all the blame to his subordinate Hai Rui from the very beginning, and finally had to admit that he was the mastermind behind the scenes under pressure
C. He initially took the initiative to expose the charges of other involved court officials, then later retracted his confession and refused to admit his previous statements
D. Cannot be judged
qa_result.answer_text: From being frightened and flustered when facing the emperor's inquiry at the beginning, passively responding and shirking responsibility, to gradually becoming resolute and tough afterwards, voluntarily taking responsibility to clear his connection with other court officials, and fully demonstrating his loyalty by standing on the side of imperial power
rationale: A. ground truth, The speaker was full of fear and dared not speak actively in the early stage of response, and his emotion gradually turned tough later. He publicly refuted Chen Hong's remarks, voluntarily took the blame and showed his loyalty to the emperor, which conforms to the only verifiable change path

B. wrong speaker, This content is the false remarks deliberately framed by other eunuchs in the conversation against the target speaker, which is not the real change track of the target speaker, and belongs to misattributing the demands of other speakers
C. hallucination, In the conversation, the speaker never made any statement of voluntarily exposing other court officials throughout the whole process, which is a completely fabricated false change
D. unknown, The changes of the speaker's emotion and attitude are supported by multiple explicit speeches, which does not belong to the situation that cannot be judged
